channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Multiple Canadian provinces pull US-made booze over Trump tariffs
item.description: Multiple Canadian provinces are responding to President Trump’s tariffs with plans to remove American alcohol from store shelves. Trump on Saturday signed off on 25 percent tariffs on Canada, with 10 percent tariffs on Canadian energy imports.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: In Kentucky bourbon country, the newest trade war feels like a hangover that won't go away
item.description: In the hills of Kentucky where most of the world’s bourbon supply is crafted, a new trade war would feel like an aching hangover that won’t go away.
item.dc:creator: Bruce Schreiner, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Another car bomb in a northern Syria city kills at least 19, mostly women
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Another car bomb exploded on the outskirts of a city in northern Syria on Monday, killing at least 19 people and leaving over a dozen wounded, hospital workers and emergency responders said.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and OMAR ALBAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France’s government is fighting for survival over its 2025 budget. Here's what to know
item.description: France's prime minister has used special executive powers to get the 2025 budget approved without a vote by lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Sylvie Corbet And Sam Petrequin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Nigeria moves to restart oil production in vulnerable region after Shell sells much of its business
item.description: The Nigerian government is in talks with local communities to restart oil production in a region that’s previously suffered environmental damage after oil giant Shell’s sale of its onshore business in the country.
item.dc:creator: Taiwo Adebayo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A blast in Moscow kills 2, including the founder of a unit fighting in eastern Ukraine, reports say
item.description: An explosion rocked an upscale residential block in Moscow early Monday, killing two people – including the founder of a battalion that battled Kyiv’s forces in eastern Ukraine -- and injuring three others, Russian authorities and news reports said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Africa hits back at Trump’s claim that it is ‘confiscating land,' as US aid to country threatened
item.description: The South African president has hit back at President Donald Trump for claiming the government is "confiscating" land. President Trump also claimed certain classes of people are being treated ‘very badly.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Their sacred land was a gift for their courage. Yet Maká people in Paraguay fight for its ownership
item.description: ASUNCIÓN, Paraguay (AP) — Many Maká traditions have slowly faded. Yet a few elders among these Paraguayan Indigenous people recall how their songs imitated birds.
item.dc:creator: MARÍA TERESA HERNÁNDEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hospitals in eastern Congo are crowded with wounded and exhausting their supplies
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Hundreds of wounded people have poured into overcrowded hospitals in Goma, a major city in eastern Congo, as fighting rages on between government forces and the Rwanda-backed rebels who seized the city of around 2 million people.
item.dc:creator: VIVIEN LATOUR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Preoccupied with its troops in Russia, North Korea unlikely to embrace Trump's overture soon
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — In its first direct criticism of the Trump administration, North Korea lashed out at U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio for calling it a “rogue” state and warned Monday that such “coarse and nonsensical remarks” will never contribute to U.S. interests.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump urged to look into US funding of Lebanese army amid accusations of its ties to Hezbollah
item.description: Amid a resurgence of Hezbollah activity on Israel’s border there are new calls for the Trump administration to push the Lebanese state to root out Hezbollah and slash aid to the country’s army.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Panama pledges to end key canal deal with China, work with US after Rubio visit
item.description: Panama's José Raúl Mulino announced that his country would not renew its agreement with China's Belt and Road Initiative following a visit from Secretary of State Marco Rubio.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UK's Starmer seeks strong trade relations with the US in the wake of Trump's tariffs
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said Sunday that he would seek a strong trade relationship with the U.S. after President Donald Trump suggested he would slap Europe with tariffs after he hit America's biggest trading partners — Canada, Mexico and China — with import taxes.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US-backed commander says his Kurdish-led group wants a secular and civil state in post-Assad Syria
item.description: HASSAKEH, Syria (AP) — The commander of the main U.S.-backed force in Syria said Sunday the recent ouster of the Assad family from power should be followed by building a secular, civil and decentralized state that treats all its citizens equally no matter their religion or ethnicity.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canadian ambassador to U.S.: Canadians 'perplexed' and 'confused' by Trump's tariffs
item.description: Canada’s ambassador to the U.S., Kirsten Hillman, said on Sunday that Canadians are “perplexed” and “confused” by tariffs enacted by President Trump. “Canadians are perplexed, I think disappointed,” Hillman told ABC News’s George Stephanopoulos.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Rubio visiting Central America with the Panama Canal and immigration top of mind
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio is on his first foreign trip in office, arriving in Central America on Saturday to press President Donald Trump's top priority — curbing illegal immigration — and to bring the message that the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he ordered airstrikes on Islamic State in Somalia
item.description: "These killers, who we found hiding in caves, threatened the United States and our Allies," Trump said in a post on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: Arab nations reject Trump’s suggestion to relocate Palestinians from Gaza to Egypt and Jordan
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Powerful Arab nations on Saturday rejected U.S. President Donald Trump’s suggestion to relocate Palestinians from Gaza to neighboring Egypt and Jordan.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump uniquely placed to ‘whisper’ in Erdogan’s ear over Turkish regional ambitions: Greek defense minister
item.description: Greek Defense Minister Nikos Dendias said NATO needs to take seriously Turkey's expansionist ambitions and President Erdoğan's desire to "recreate the Ottoman Empire" amid aggression.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As M23 rebel group advances in Congo, a new leader signals a shift in its identity
item.description: KAMPALA, Uganda (AP) — After Rwanda-backed M23 rebels took control of the biggest city in eastern Congo this week, the man who emerged from the shadows to assert his leadership was not the group's long-time military leader. Sultani Makenga, an ethnic Tutsi rebel leader sanctioned by both the U.S.
item.dc:creator: RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas releases 3 more hostages, including US citizen, as part of fragile ceasefire deal
item.description: Hamas on Saturday freed three more hostages, including American-Israeli Keith Siegel, in the latest phase of a fragile ceasefire with Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran's covert nuclear agency found operating out of top space program launch sites
item.description: Sources embedded in Iran uncover "large scale" personnel and operations affiliated with Iran's nuclear program are now operating out of two of Tehran's top space development and testing sites.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israelis and Palestinians rejoice after more hostages and prisoners are freed
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Eight more hostages were freed from the Gaza Strip by Hamas-led militants on Thursday in a sometimes chaotic process that briefly delayed Israel's release of 110 Palestinian prisoners and underscored the fragility of the ceasefire that began earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH, WAFAA SHURAFA and ISABEL DeBRE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu furious about chaotic handover of Israeli hostages from Hamas
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu ordered a delay in the planned release of terrorists, seeking assurance regarding the release of hostages, but the prime minister's office later indicated that the demand had been satisfied.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Moment IDF soldier is reunited with family seen on video
item.description: Agam Berger, an IDF soldier who was taken hostage during Hamas' attacks on Oct. 7, 2023, is reunited with her family after spending 482 days in captivity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korean leader Kim inspects nuclear facility as Pyongyang pressures Trump administration
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un inspected a facility that produces nuclear material and called for bolstering the country’s nuclear fighting capability, state media reported Wednesday, as the North looks to increase pressure on the United States following the inauguration o
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump invites Israel's Netanyahu to meet with him at the White House next week
item.description: WADI GAZA, Gaza Strip (AP) — President Donald Trump has invited Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to the White House next week as the first foreign leader to visit in Trump's second term, Netanhayu and the White House said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: CDC ordered to stop working with WHO immediately, upending expectations of an extended withdrawal
item.description: President Donald Trump last week issued an executive order to begin the process of withdrawing the U.S. from WHO, but that did not take immediate effect.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, NewsNation
